response.justification: Candidate is an experienced senior engineer with strong full-stack and systems background, but the resume shows minimal recent Java/Kotlin/Spring work and no demonstrated experience with IDE internals or JVM-based language tooling. Primary expertise is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, which does not align with the core requirements of building an enterprise JVM IDE. Java appears only briefly in older roles (robotic arms, mobile orchestration) without depth in Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or build-tool/app-server integration.
strengths: Strong senior-level experience across complex, large-scale systems and microservices
Prior IDE-adjacent work at National Instruments (C/C++ for LabWindows/CVI)
Solid software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools, open-source contributions
concerns: No demonstrated deep expertise in Kotlin or modern Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate ecosystems
Recent stack is TypeScript/Node and DLang, not JVM — limited current JVM depth
No evidence of work on language tooling, parsers, or IDE internals at the depth required

key_factors.factor: No IDE internals or language-tooling experience
Senior engineering breadth and prior C/C++ IDE work
